rationale: Recent negative exchange/protocol batch covering DMM Bitcoin, BtcTurk, and GMX. DMM Bitcoin is modeled as dead / hack because the 2024 BTC theft led to asset/account transfer to SBI VC Trade and final service termination in March 2025. BtcTurk is modeled as active with critical suspicious-outflow / withdrawal-suspension events because reporting describes hot-wallet losses and temporary crypto deposit/withdrawal suspension while trading and fiat functions continued. GMX is modeled as active with GMX v1 exploit / GLP and v1 trading-pause events because GMX v2 and the broader protocol continued after the July 2025 exploit.

entities.summary: Japanese centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suffered a 2024 unauthorized BTC outflow and later transferred customer accounts/assets to SBI VC Trade before ending service on March 8, 2025.
entities.notes: Classified as dead / hack because the terminal service end followed the unauthorized BTC outflow and customer asset/account transfer to SBI VC Trade. The exchange did not simply rebrand; service ended under the DMM Bitcoin entity.

entities.summary: Turkish centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suspended crypto deposits and withdrawals in August 2025 after suspicious hot-wallet outflows reported around USD 48-49 million, while stating that fiat functions and trading continued and that user assets were safe.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The incident was a major negative CEX event but sources describe temporary crypto deposit/withdrawal suspension, continued trading/TRY operations, and user-asset safety statements rather than a terminal shutdown.

entities.summary: Decentralized perpetual exchange protocol. In July 2025, GMX v1 / GLP on Arbitrum was exploited for about USD 42 million, leading to disabled v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem on Arbitrum and Avalanche while GMX v2 and the broader protocol continued.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The exploit affected GMX v1 / GLP and triggered temporary v1/GLP disablement, while GMX v2 was reported as unaffected and the broader protocol continued.

events.title: DMM Bitcoin suffered an unauthorized BTC outflow
events.description: DMM Bitcoin suffered an unauthorized outflow of bitcoin in 2024, widely reported as approximately 4,502.9 BTC and roughly JPY 48 billion at the time.
events.notes: Use hack because the outflow is the causative security incident behind the later transfer and shutdown.

events.title: DMM Bitcoin announced customer transfer to SBI VC Trade
events.description: DMM Bitcoin announced an agreement to transfer customer accounts and assets to SBI VC Trade, with the transfer path replacing a restart of DMM Bitcoin's own exchange service.
events.notes: This is the practical shutdown-announcement marker; use official DMM/SBI sources for final staging validation.

events.title: DMM Bitcoin service ended after SBI VC Trade transfer
events.description: DMM Bitcoin service ended on March 8, 2025 after customer assets and accounts were transferred to SBI VC Trade.
events.notes: HEI uses the official service-end / transfer completion date as the terminal marker.

events.title: BtcTurk saw suspicious hot-wallet outflows
events.description: BtcTurk experienced suspicious outflows from hot wallets in August 2025, with blockchain/security reporting and news sources estimating the affected value around USD 48-49 million.
events.notes: Use careful language because some sources frame it as suspicious outflows / hack fears, while the exchange described security measures and user-asset safety.

events.title: BtcTurk suspended crypto deposits and withdrawals
events.description: BtcTurk suspended cryptocurrency deposits and withdrawals after the suspicious hot-wallet activity, while reports indicated Turkish-lira services and trading remained available.
events.notes: Temporary crypto-transfer suspension; not a shutdown marker.

events.title: GMX v1 / GLP was exploited for about USD 42 million
events.description: GMX v1 / GLP on Arbitrum was exploited for about USD 42 million, with the attacker later offered a white-hat bounty and some funds reportedly returned.
events.notes: Use exploit because this was a protocol/liquidity-pool exploit, not a centralized exchange wallet hack.

events.title: GMX disabled v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem
events.description: GMX disabled GMX v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem functionality on Arbitrum and Avalanche after the exploit, while stating or reporting that GMX v2 was unaffected.
events.notes: Temporary/partial protocol disablement; keep entity active because the broader GMX protocol continued.

events.title: GMX recovery process began after white-hat negotiations
events.description: Follow-up reporting described a white-hat bounty / return-of-funds process after the GMX exploit, supporting active classification despite the serious v1 incident.
events.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no white-hat recovery enum.

evidence.title: SBI VC Trade account/asset transfer information for DMM Bitcoin customers
evidence.notes: Primary transfer-destination source for DMM Bitcoin customer account/asset migration and service transition details.

evidence.title: DMM Bitcoin notice on transfer of customer accounts/assets to SBI VC Trade
evidence.notes: Primary DMM source for the post-incident transfer path and service-end planning.

evidence.title: SBI VC Trade completed transfer of all customer accounts/assets from DMM Bitcoin
evidence.notes: Used for transfer completion / service transition date.

evidence.title: DMM Bitcoin will transfer assets to SBI VC Trade, then shut down
evidence.notes: Supporting source linking the May 2024 unauthorized BTC outflow to the asset-transfer / shutdown path.

evidence.title: Turkish crypto exchange BtcTurk witnesses $48M of suspicious outflows amid hack fears
evidence.notes: Used for suspicious-outflow amount, exchange identity, and hack-fears context.

evidence.title: Turkey's oldest crypto exchange BtcTurk halts deposits, withdrawals after $48 million in unusual outflows
evidence.notes: Used for deposit/withdrawal halt and continued trading/TRY operations.

evidence.title: Crypto platform BtcTurk says all security measures taken after claims of $30M hacking
evidence.notes: Supporting source for BtcTurk's user-asset safety and service-scope statements.

evidence.title: Decentralized Exchange GMX Exploited for $42M, Offers Hacker 10% White-Hat Bounty
evidence.notes: Used for exploit amount, GLP/v1 context, and white-hat bounty offer.

evidence.title: Hacker drains $42 million from decentralized perpetual exchange GMX
evidence.notes: Used for v1 trading and GLP mint/redeem disablement on Arbitrum/Avalanche and v2-unaffected context.

evidence.title: GMX Crypto Exchange Hacked for $42 Million as Token Crashes
evidence.notes: Supporting source for recovery / return-of-funds context and market impact.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm DMM Bitcoin, BtcTurk, and GMX are not already canonical before apply; if existing, update rather than duplicate.
Apply DMM Bitcoin as dead / hack with 2025-03-08 terminal service-end marker.
Apply BtcTurk as active with suspicious-outflow / withdrawal-suspended events; do not mark dead.
Apply GMX as active with GMX v1 / GLP exploit and v1 trading halt events; do not mark dead because v2 and the broader protocol continued.
Use event ids hei_ev_000402 through hei_ev_000409 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000811 through hei_src_000820 only if available.
